title: torilis arvensis subsp. elongata
intro: Torilis arvensis subsp. elongata, also known as the long-stemmed hedge parsley or hollow stem burnet, is a subspecies of the Torilis arvensis plant, a type of flowering plant in the carrot family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Torilis arvensis subsp. elongata include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever and pain, and support the respiratory system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be made by drying and processing the leaves and stems to utilize its medicinal properties.
